title: City of Chaos - Let's Play - Part 3
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4n-UnjzY3B0

Daniel (@dungeondive) tightens his bookkeeping for City of Chaos — logging read paragraphs and drawing location cards as tiles are placed. Bing returns to the gift shop and buys the x-ray spectacles, which hand him his first clue card (#8): they'll reveal a hidden door in the Marble Minaret. He re-reads the corpse passage, piecing together that the dead duelist Sif feuded with the Pyrotechnics, and resolves to march Dizzy to the Duelists' Academy with the ring.

That journey, however, is cursed. Dizzy buys a disguise (Whirly Whiskers) from a shady spy, then is goaded into the game's first combat by a sailor who shoves him into a sewer — and the brutal, deadly system kills them both simultaneously, sending Dizzy back to the city gates. Bing dodges an assassin's offer and gets caught in a roving "chaos pocket" that rotates the streets, leaving Dizzy stranded further than ever — only to be swept up by the Wild Hunt, compelled to roam and slay the first creature he meets.

A run of bad luck for Dizzy against Bing's steady clue-gathering — the classic one-player-thrives dynamic of these games.

----

The combat here can kill both fighters at once — does a deadly, swingy combat system add tension for you, or just frustration?
